content: App passwords are created using your email providers webmail or account settings. Exact steps vary between providers.
creator: platform34

content: Ok, but i was expecting the android app to provide a master password just like the PC version does!?
creator: t.hill3

content:
Ah, ok.

If i'm understanding correctly, It sounds like your asking about a lock mechanism that prompts for a password or biometrics before unlocking the app for use.

That feature is not currently available within the app.

It has been requested by community members on Mozilla connect. You can show support by voting for the idea linked below.

https://connect.mozilla.org/t5/ideas/biometrics-to-open-thunderbird-android/idi-p/74741

A community developer has submitted code to add the feature and is in the backlog of changes for review.

https://github.com/thunderbird/thunderbird-android/pull/10539

FYI, in the context of email applications, "App password" is an alternative authentication method used when connecting to your email provider, hence the confusion.
creator: platform34

content: I found the answer myself. I need to sort the emails by importance.
creator: golovin.axel

content: Glad you managed to resolve the issue, I assume by sorting by starred emails first.
creator: platform34

content:
Hi Stixman

Thunderbird for Android can only download emails that are available on the server. Emails stored only within local folders on TB desktop (no copy on the server) cannot be viewed in the mobile app.
